projectDescription: A free and open-source C++ library for GPU-accelerated vector tile map rendering on Android, iOS, Node.js, and other native platforms.
keyFeatures: GPU-accelerated vector tile rendering via OpenGL, Metal, Vulkan, and WebGPU backends
Cross-platform support for Android, iOS, Linux, Windows, macOS, Node.js, and Qt
Runtime styling API for dynamic map style manipulation
Offline maps with SQLite-based tile caching
MapLibre Style Specification compliance with expressions support
Modular renderer architecture supporting multiple graphics APIs
Immutable style diffing for efficient frame-to-frame updates
Multi-threaded tile loading and layout with actor-based message passing
publicApiSurface: mbgl::Map — main map control class (camera, style, annotations, debug)
mbgl::style::Style — runtime style manipulation (layers, sources, images)
mbgl::style::Layer and subclasses (BackgroundLayer, CircleLayer, FillLayer, LineLayer, SymbolLayer, RasterLayer, HeatmapLayer, HillshadeLayer, FillExtrusionLayer, CustomLayer)
mbgl::style::Source and subclasses (VectorSource, RasterSource, GeoJSONSource, ImageSource, RasterDEMSource, CustomGeometrySource)
mbgl::ResourceOptions, mbgl::ClientOptions — network and client configuration
@maplibre/maplibre-gl-native npm package (Node.js): mbgl.Map, map.load(), map.render(), map.release()
